heading: Program for public outdoor recreation
label: 670c Program for public outdoor recreation

children.heading: Program authorized
children.label: a Program authorized
children.text: The Secretary of Defense is also authorized to carry out a program for the development, enhancement, operation, and maintenance of public outdoor recreation resources at military installations in accordance with an integrated natural resources management plan mutually agreed upon by the Secretary of Defense and the Secretary of the Interior, in consultation with the appropriate State agency designated by the State in which the installations are located.

children.heading: Access for disabled veterans, military dependents with disabilities, and other persons with disabilities
children.label: b Access for disabled veterans, military dependents with disabilities, and other persons with disabilities

children.text: In developing facilities and conducting programs for public outdoor recreation at military installations, consistent with the primary military mission of the installations, the Secretary of Defense shall ensure, to the extent reasonably practicable, that outdoor recreation opportunities (including fishing, hunting, trapping, wildlife viewing, boating, and camping) made available to the public also provide access for persons described in paragraph (2) when topographic, vegetative, and water resources allow access for such persons without substantial modification to the natural environment.

children.label: C
children.text: Other persons with disabilities, when access to a military installation for such persons and other civilians is not otherwise restricted.

The Secretary of Defense shall carry out this subsection in consultation with the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, national service, military, and veterans organizations, and sporting organizations in the private sector that participate in outdoor recreation projects for persons described in paragraph (2).
the voluntary services of individuals and organizations; and
donations of property, whether real or personal.
for the purposes of the tort claims provisions of chapter 171 of title 28, the volunteer shall be considered to be a Federal employee; and

for the purposes of subchapter I of chapter 81 of title 5, relating to compensation to Federal employees for work injuries, the volunteer shall be considered to be an employee, as defined in , and the provisions of such subchapter shall apply.section 8101(1)(B) of title 5
